name: Mjuk Pepparkaka or Swedish Spice Bread
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan, or two 7 1/2 x 3 1/2 x 2-inch pans.
In a large mixing bowl, beat together the sugar and butter with an electric mixer until fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Add the dry ingredients and sour cream alternately to the beaten mixture, beating until just combined.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan(s).
Bake for 55 to 60 minutes in the large loaf, or 45 to 50 minutes for the smaller loaves.
Cool in the pan(s) on wire racks for 10 minutes. Remove; cool completely.
Makes 1 or 2 loaves.
Note: Serve this pound-cakelike bread with whipped cream laced with shredded lemon peel. Or you can slice the loaf into three layers horizontally and fill with whipped cream.
